title: Adding Mirror Columns In Bulk
excerpt:
I have successfully connected a board and mirrored my 1st item.  How can I extend this to the next 500 existing items in the board, without manually mapping each one? 
Seems like it must be a basic thing?  I would love t…

posts.author: iwoodford (Ian Woodford)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have successfully connected a board and mirrored my 1st item. How can I extend this to the next 500 existing items in the board, without manually mapping each one?

Seems like it must be a basic thing? I would love to then add additional mirror columns too.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Type in Match in automation templates. I usually create a status column for this. when status changes to done, match and connect this column with this column for board X. (something along those lines). Example is matching a lead’s email address to a contact record in CRM. You can select 500 items at a time to do this ‘connect’.

Bruce Gosk | bruce@mintconsulting.org

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: What Bruce said is right, just keep in mind that this does take an automation so if you’re on Pro, that could use up a chunk of your allotment.

posts.author: iwoodford (Ian Woodford)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just wanted to thank you both for your suggestions above. I continue to be surprised at how complicated a relatively easy update is on this platform. I will instead export these boards, do a basic vLookup in excel, then reimport them.

The frustrating thing is that Monday.com does not allow imports with groupings so I will have to get creative to retain them.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @iwoodford

We’ve developed an app called Connected Boards Automations. Which allows to run robust automations on connected boards column.

In this case you can connect items based on automation logic, just like vLookup in Excel.

Automatically connect items based on one or multiple criteria →
